Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-10-2019 Continued To Jul-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Mancuso for Executor.
Amanda Ebey for Romula Villanueva (Creditor/Heir).

Mr. Mancuso stated that the civil matter has a bearing on heirship, suggested setting a status hearing after the trial, stated that he received 44 consents for his heirship petition and that he found that the percentages are incorrect. Mr. Mancuso also suggested that his petition go off calendar and stated that he can re-file with the corrected percentages. Ms. Ebey stated that they need to know who will be entitled to distribution and that the petition does not give any assistance to the Court as to who they are. Ms. Ebey's position is that there are 18 co-equal heirs. Mr. Mancuso's position is that there are 64 heirs. Ms. Ebey requested that the Court issue a full order with findings of fact. The Court stated that this is not an evidentiary hearing and that it is a legal question. Ms. Ebey requested an evidentiary hearing. The Petition to Determine Persons Entitled to Distribution of Estate is continued to July 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an evidentiary hearing. Witness lists and exhibit list are to be filed one week before the hearing.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Appearances: Peter Mancuso for and with Santos Cadiente (Personal Representative).
Jeff Handwerger (Proposed Buyer) also present.

Mr. Handwerger stated that he waives any issues with regard to service. The sale was confirmed at $2,455,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt and review of the order). The proceeds from the sale are to be deposited into several blocked accounts in lieu of bond. Mr. Mancuso stated that the net proceeds from the sale will be $2,000,000.00, that the Heritage Bank of Commerce in San Jose will take control of the funds and make deposits to several other banks, and that the funds will be insured at each bank for $250,000.00. The Court informed Counsel that the order needs to include the names of all of the banks were the funds are being deposited.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Status on filing of the receipts for the blocked accounts is set for September 12, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court Clerk will notify Counsel of the hearing date.
